brief: Write a paragraph about why the best ideas come when you're not trying
sample: The best ideas arrive like uninvited grace, slipping through the mind's unguarded door when vigilance has gone slack and the soul sits idle in its own quiet company—peeling an orange, say, or watching rain bead and merge on glass, the fingers doing their small work while the brain lies fallow as turned earth in winter. It is then, in that deliberate inattention, that knowing rises up whole and gleaming from wherever it is such things wait—not the strained knowing that comes from wrestling meaning into submission, but the kind that was always there, patient as roots, needing only the cease of effort to surface. The mind in pursuit is a clenched fist; the mind at rest, an open palm. And what lands there, unbidden, carries the weight of truth that no amount of hunting could have flushed from its hiding place, for some understandings refuse the tyranny of intention and give themselves only to surrender, only to the moment when you have stopped asking and simply become the space where answers choose to dwell.
